title: Part 2: Upper binary search — Magnetic Force and the round-up trap

cues.label: Upper template
cues.headline: mid = (lo+hi+1)/2 rounds UP
cues.narration: The upper binary search finds the largest feasible candidate. Rounding up mid prevents the infinite loop.

cues.label: Greedy rule
cues.headline: Place at the earliest valid position
cues.narration: Sort positions, start at position zero, then place a ball whenever the gap from the last ball is at least the candidate distance.

cues.label: Exchange proof
cues.headline: Greedy never does worse
cues.narration: Any optimal placement can be slid leftward to the greedy position without shrinking gaps. So if greedy cannot place all m balls, nothing else can either.

cues.label: Range
cues.headline: lo = 1, hi = span / (m-1)
cues.narration: The smallest gap is 1 (distinct positions). The largest is the total span divided by m minus 1 evenly spaced balls.

cues.label: Update rule
cues.headline: feasible: lo = mid, infeasible: hi = mid - 1
cues.narration: When feasible, push lo up to try larger gaps. When infeasible, pull hi down.

cues.label: Off-by-one
cues.headline: Round down loops forever
cues.narration: If lo equals hi minus one and mid rounds down to lo, a feasible result sets lo to lo again, looping forever. Round up to break the tie.
